text:
During a chilly afternoon walk in the park, a group of children found a peculiar old tree stump. They got wild ideas about what they could create out of it. Sally insisted they create a tree house.

They spent the whole day digging holes, collecting sticks, and lining them up to make branches. The next day, they climbed their tree house only to discover it was half hollow. Heartbroken, the kids carved windows and hung ropes.

They slept inside one night, nibbling on cookies Sally brought. They dreamed of adventures.

Later the next day, they tried to fly. They'd lean out too far and flap their arms.

After they chased each other around for hours, they decided the tree stump was perfectly flat for a pancake house. They sat down, cracked eggs, fried pancakes, and ate them under the moonlit sky.

They laughed all night long—kids do. Those trees are now their tree house.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
